page: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server hosted on the QEMU hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical AMD EPYC-Rome cores running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of an 80 GB SSD, and there are no dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25 percent at 2672.38. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is average, with read-only large block speeds at 67.66 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 49.67 GB/sec. However, the Passmark memory score is weak, placing in the bottom 25 percent. Software compilation with Clang and static web serving throughput are also weak, with the latter reaching 6.15 GB/sec.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff, as resources are shared with other tenants, which explains the average multi-core and weak memory scores. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a dense allocation of 4 physical cores and local SSD storage at an entry-level hardware class, making it a viable option for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. Suitable workloads include lightweight database operations, text processing, basic Redis caching, and small-scale LLM inference using models under 1B parameters.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs on AMD EPYC-Rome processors running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain average. Tradeoffs include a weak Passmark memory score and lower throughput for software compilation and static web serving. Given its shared CPU allocation, this server provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring moderate compute density without dedicated resources. The platform utilizes the QEMU hypervisor to manage virtualized resources, delivering a balanced environment for non-critical applications that benefit from multi-core availability without the premium cost of dedicated hardware threads. It is suited for lightweight database operations, text processing, Redis caching, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage.
tagline: A cost-efficient shared-core virtual server featuring four AMD EPYC cores and local SSD storage for moderate compute workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Weak memory performance and software compilation speeds
Best suited for lightweight databases, Redis caching, and text processing
